summary: Gruber lays out his prediction that Apple's September 2015 'Hey Siri' event will focus on iPhones, Apple Watch, and a new Apple TV — but not new iPads, contrary to reports from Paczkowski and Gurman. He argues that cramming iPads into an already-packed event would shortchange every product, and that a two-event fall strategy (September for phones/TV/Watch, October for iPads/Macs/El Capitan) makes far more sense. He notes the iPad Pro's November ship date as further evidence for a later announcement. He acknowledges he could be wrong and engages with reader counterarguments, but maintains the one-event scenario would be 'inexplicable.'
key_points: The September event already has three major segments (iPhones, Apple Watch, Apple TV) that each demand significant stage time, leaving no room for iPads without rushing introductions.
Apple has held separate September and October events since 2012, and the iPad Pro — a major new form factor — deserves its own spotlight rather than being sandwiched between other announcements.
The iPad Pro's reported November ship date suggests Apple would follow its pattern of not pre-announcing products in existing categories months before availability.
A two-event strategy would also preserve stage time for Mac hardware and El Capitan, which would otherwise get no fall event coverage.
Gruber openly acknowledges going against well-sourced reporters and engages honestly with counterarguments from readers.
money_quotes: Not only does the two-event scenario seem more likely to me, I'd go so far as to call the one-event scenario inexplicable. Possible, sure. But not logical.
These new iPads would get more attention headlining their own (albeit smaller) event than they will if they get sandwiched between new iPhones and Apple TV.
The new Apple TV is an altogether new platform — with a radical new remote and a full-on SDK and App Store. That will necessarily consume a lot of on-stage demo time.
Conspicuously absent from my guess is anything related to new iPads. I have no sources for this, no hints from any little birdies. This is just my gut feeling.

Occasionally, such as with the original iPhone and last year's Apple Watch, Apple will unveil a new product months ahead of it hitting stores. But the iPhone and Apple Watch were brand new product categories.
It's certainly possible that Apple could include the new iPads in this event, but there's no way they could do the iPads and Mac/El Capitan all in this one event.
key_insight: Apple's product announcements work best when each major product gets the stage time it deserves, and cramming too many launches into one event shortchanges them all.
